channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Startup Battlefield Australia Extends Application Deadline to July 20
item.description: TechCrunch's Startup Battlefield Australia has extended its application deadline to July 20, 2026, due to overwhelming interest. This extension provides late-stage applicants with a final opportunity ...

item.title: Six-Year-Old Boy Found Dead in Karachi After Alleged Sexual Assault and Murder
item.description: A six-year-old boy was allegedly sexually assaulted and murdered after being abducted from his residence in Karachi's Napier area. Police confirmed the arrest of Hamza, the victim's neighbor, who was ...

item.title: DepEd Implements Safety Measures and Psychological Support Following Campus Violence
item.description: The Department of Education (DepEd) has announced the implementation of active shooter drills in schools nationwide as part of enhanced safety protocols following recent campus violence incidents. Edu...

item.title: Preliminary Hearing for Tyler Robinson in Charlie Kirk's Murder Case
item.description: The article details the ongoing preliminary hearing for Tyler Robinson, accused of murdering conservative activist Charlie Kirk on a Utah university campus. Forensic evidence, including DNA samples fr...

item.title: New Rugby Laws Promote Open Play and Contested Matches
item.description: Springbok coach Rassie Erasmus has praised the latest rugby law changes, calling the resulting gameplay 'beautiful rugby'. The inaugural Nations Championship tournament saw 54 tries in the opening rou...

item.title: Australian War Memorial Launches Enhanced Membership Program to Preserve National History
item.description: The Australian War Memorial has introduced a refreshed membership program offering exclusive access to its hidden collections and vital history preservation initiatives. This initiative expands beyond...

item.title: Civil Society Urges Adequate Funding for FRLD Amid Climate Crisis
item.description: The 9th board meeting of the Fund for Responding to Loss and Damage (FRLD) is ongoing in Manila, with civil society organizations (CSOs), indigenous groups, and climate advocates raising concerns over...

item.title: Nyahururu Water and Sanitation Company Launches Job Recruitment Drive for Kenyans
item.description: Nyahururu Water and Sanitation Company Limited has opened applications for five key positions across finance, commercial, supply chain, and sewerage departments. The roles include a finance manager, c...

item.title: Trump Intervenes in Cabinet Dispute Over Military Flyover for Todd Blanche
item.description: President Donald Trump intervened in a Cabinet dispute to allow Acting U.S. Attorney General Todd Blanche to participate in a military flyover for NASA administrator Jared Isaacman's private jet colle...

item.title: Dar es Salaam Experiences Varying Weather Conditions Across Different Areas
item.description: Dar es Salaam, Tanzania's largest city with over six million residents, is facing diverse weather patterns across its neighborhoods. Some areas report clear skies and sunny conditions, while others ex...

item.title: Toronto police officers charged in major corruption probe face direct trial in Superior Court
item.description: Toronto Police Constable Timothy Barnhardt and six other officers are facing charges in the Project South corruption probe, which involves allegations of organized crime ties and misconduct. The Crown...

item.title: Top Roblox players by in-game wealth and achievements
item.description: The article highlights the top 10 Roblox players ranked by their in-game wealth, measured through RAP values (Roblox Asset Points). David Baszucki, the founder of Roblox, is noted as the first billion...

item.title: Senate opens Sara Duterte impeachment trial with key developments
item.description: The Senate convened as an impeachment court on July 8, 2026, to formally open the impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte. This 92-day proceeding marked a historic moment in Philippine politi...

item.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.6 After Delay Amid National Security Concerns
item.description: OpenAI, the US-based artificial intelligence research lab, has announced the public launch of its most advanced model, GPT-5.6, set for Thursday. The delay in the release followed government requests ...

item.title: SpaceX Shares Drop Despite Nasdaq-100 Inclusion Amid Market Volatility
item.description: SpaceX shares fell to their lowest-ever closing price on Tuesday despite the company’s recent inclusion in the Nasdaq-100 index. The stock slid 6.8% to $149.47, more than 25% below its June 16 high of...

item.title: Political Interference in Football: FIFA Faces Criticism Over Trump's Involvement
item.description: The 2026 World Cup has been plagued by political controversies that overshadow the sporting events. Initial issues included visa denials for officials from certain countries and a Somali referee barre...

item.title: Woden man wins $40 million Powerball jackpot after unregistered ticket
item.description: A man from Woden has claimed a $40 million Powerball jackpot after his winning ticket remained unregistered for weeks. The winner, an ACT resident, only discovered his fortune when news reports highli...

item.title: US-Iran Tensions Escalate Over Strait of Hormuz Attacks and Maritime Security Disputes
item.description: The article details the escalating conflict between the United States and Iran following Iran's attacks on commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz. The U.S. has launched retaliatory strikes against...

item.title: Trump Launches Iran Strikes Amid NATO Summit Focused on Defense Spending
item.description: During the NATO summit in Ankara, President Donald Trump surprised allies by launching military strikes against Iran following attacks on merchant ships in the Strait of Hormuz. The U.S. actions disru...

item.title: Protesters urge Senate to delay Fasina’s ambassadorial confirmation over legal issues
item.description: Scores of demonstrators stormed the National Assembly in Abuja, calling on the Senate to suspend the confirmation of Prof Abayomi Fasina as a non-career ambassador. The protests stem from allegations ...
